name: The s-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

tests.testPaperName: The s Block Elements CBSE Online Test 01

questions.question: The s-block elements of the Periodic Table have only:
questions.question_raw: The s-block elements of the Periodic Table have only:
questions.explanation: S-block comprises of only two groups alkali metal group and alkaline earth metal groups.

questions.question: Group I elements of the periodic table are collectively known as:
questions.question_raw: Group I elements of the periodic table are collectively known as:
questions.explanation: Group I (ns^1) elemens react vigorously with water to produce an alkaline solution thus collectively known as alkali metals.

questions.question: Group IlA of the periodic table consists of the six elements are collectively known as _________.
questions.question_raw: Group IlA of the periodic table consists of the six elements are collectively known as _________.
questions.explanation: Group IlA of the periodic table consists of the six elements are collectively known as alkaline earth metals because their oxide and hydroxide are alkaline in nature and these metal oxide are found in earth's crust.

questions.question: Which one of the following is not called alkaline earth metal?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is not called alkaline earth metal?
questions.explanation:
Alkaline earth metals are called so because of two reasons
 Alkaline earth metals are obtained on earth in the form of ores
 Oxides and hydroxides of Alkaline earth metals are basic in nature,
However, Beryllium fulfills 1st reason but its oxides and hydroxides are amphoteric so sometimes they say it is not alkaline earth metal. but as it is amphoteric IUPAC has accepted this as alkaline earth metal.

questions.question: Calcium and Magnesium belong to the category of:
questions.question_raw: Calcium and Magnesium belong to the category of:
questions.explanation: Calcium and magnesium are 2^nd group element of the periodic table which are obtained on earth in the form ore, their oxides and hydroxide are alkaline in nature so they are alkaline earth metals.

questions.question: Sodium and potassium belong to the category of:
questions.question_raw: Sodium and potassium belong to the category of:
questions.explanation: Na and K are shiny, soft, highly reactive metal at standard temperature and pressure and electronic configuration of Na & K is ns^2, compounds of Na & K are available most abundantly in nature so these two are alkali metal.

questions.question: Which one of the following is highly radioactive?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is highly radioactive?
questions.explanation: Fr is a highly radioactive metal, that decays into astatine, radium, and radon. It is also highly unstable element found only in trace amount on earth.

questions.question: The correct sequence in decreasing order of the percentage of nitrogen in the given compounds is
questions.question_raw: The correct sequence in decreasing order of the percentage of nitrogen in the given compounds is

options.label: A
options.text: Urea > Ammonium chloride > Ammonium nitrate > Ammonium nitrite

options.label: B
options.text: Urea > Ammonium nitrite > Ammonium chloride > Ammonium nitrate

options.label: C
options.text: Urea > Ammonium nitrate > Ammonium nitrite > Ammonium chloride

options.label: D
options.text: Urea > Ammonium nitrite > Ammonium nitrate > Ammonium chloride

correct_option_texts: Urea > Ammonium nitrite > Ammonium nitrate > Ammonium chloride
questions.explanation: Correct sequence is $N H _ { 2 } C O N H _ { 2 } > N H _ { 4 } N _ { 3 } > N H _ { 3 } N O _ { 3 } > N H _ { 4 } C l$

questions.question: Increase in ionic radii of alkali metals in aqueous solution (hydration) follow the order:
questions.question_raw: Increase in ionic radii of alkali metals in aqueous solution (hydration) follow the order:
questions.explanation: The alkali metal ions get extensively hydrated in aqueous solution. Smaller the ion more is the degree of hydration.

questions.question: Monovalent sodium and potassium ions found in large proportions in biological fluids. They help in:
questions.question_raw: Monovalent sodium and potassium ions found in large proportions in biological fluids. They help in:
questions.explanation: Na and K ions help in the transmission of nerve impulses, in regulating the flow of water across the cell membranes, and in the transport of sugar and amino acids into cells. so monovalent sodium and potassium ions found in large proportions in biological fluids.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate one that shows the hydration enthalpies of alkali metal ions in the decreasing order is.
questions.question_raw: Choose the appropriate one that shows the hydration enthalpies of alkali metal ions in the decreasing order is.
questions.explanation: As the size increases positive charge density decreases so hydration energy also decreases. Hydration varies inversely with the size.

questions.question: Divalent magnesium and calcium ions are found in large proportions in biological fluids. They help in:
questions.question_raw: Divalent magnesium and calcium ions are found in large proportions in biological fluids. They help in:
questions.explanation: Monovalent sodium and potassium ions and divalent magnesium and calcium ions are found in large proportions in biological fluids. These ions perform important biological functions such as maintenance of ion balance and nerve impulse conduction.

questions.question: Which one of the following is the rarest of all alkaline earth metals?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is the rarest of all alkaline earth metals?
questions.explanation: Ra is found in Uranium and Thorium ores in trace amounts as small as a seventh of a gram per ton of uraninite. So, it is rarest of all alkaline earth metals.

questions.question: Among the alkali metals, the most abundant metals are:
questions.question_raw: Among the alkali metals, the most abundant metals are:
questions.explanation: Na and K are highly abundant. Constituting 2.5 and 2.4 percent of the Earth's crust respectively the other alkali metals are considerably rare.

questions.question: The general electronic configuration of alkali metals is:
questions.question_raw: The general electronic configuration of alkali metals is:
questions.explanation: The general electronic configuration of alkali metals is ns^1 as their outermost electron enters the s orbital and they have only on an electron in their valence shell.

tests.testPaperName: The s Block Elements CBSE Online Test 02
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > The s-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: The general electronic configuration of ns^2 exists in:
questions.question_raw: The general electronic configuration of ns2 exists in:
questions.explanation: The general electronic configuration of ns^2 exists in alkaline earth metals as their outer most electron enter the S orbital and they have only two electrons in their valence shell.

questions.question: The alkali metals and their salts impart characteristic colour to an oxidizing flame because:
questions.question_raw: The alkali metals and their salts impart characteristic colour to an oxidizing flame because:

options.label: A
options.text: there is emission of radiation in the visible region from the excited electron

options.label: B
options.text: sufficient light energy is absorbed to make an atom gain an electron

options.label: C
options.text: the smallest atomic size makes them to absorb radiation

options.label: D
options.text: the largest atomic size makes them to emit radiation

correct_option_texts: there is emission of radiation in the visible region from the excited electron
questions.explanation: Alkali metals have one unpaired electron in their valence shell which gets excited by absorbing the energy from the flame, excited states are very unstable and quickly move back to a lower state, at that time they emit the energy in the form of radiation in visible reason of electromagnetic spectrum and is observed as characteristic colour.

questions.question: Flame photometry is used to determine the concentration of the:
questions.question_raw: Flame photometry is used to determine the concentration of the:
questions.explanation: Flame photometry is used to determine the concentration of alkali metal ions, alkaline earth metals are quite sensitive to flame photometry.

questions.question: The electronic configuration of Rn7s^1 belongs to:
questions.question_raw: The electronic configuration of Rn7s1 belongs to:
questions.explanation: Francium will fill orbitals all the way up to 7s (7^th period) subshell where it will have half-filled orbital: 7s^1.

questions.question: The alkali metals burn vigorously in oxygen forming oxides. Hence, potassium forms ________.
questions.question_raw: The alkali metals burn vigorously in oxygen forming oxides. Hence, potassium forms ________.
questions.explanation: Alkali metals burn vigorously to form superoxides. Superoxide O_2^- ion is formed only in the presence of large cations such as K, Rb, and Cs.

questions.question: What is the oxidation state of K in KO_2?
questions.question_raw: What is the oxidation state of K in KO2?
questions.explanation: K has +1 oxidation state. "The potassium atom losses one electron when forming compound as it has one electron on it's valence shell".

questions.question: ${\text{2M }} + {\text{ 2}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{2}}{{\text{M}}^ + } + {\text{ 2O}}{{\text{H}}^ - } + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}$. What is M?
questions.question_raw: {tex}{\text{2M }} + {\text{ 2}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O }} \to {\text{2}}{{\text{M}}^ + } + {\text{ 2O}}{{\text{H}}^ - } + {\text{ }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{/tex}. What is M?
questions.explanation: M is alkali metal. Alkali metals react with water vigorously to form hydroxide (OH^-) and dihydrogen (H_2).

questions.question: Lithium has the most negative E^0 value, its reaction with water is less vigorous than that of sodium which has the least negative E^0 value among the alkali metals. The reason is:
questions.question_raw: Lithium has the most negative E0 value, its reaction with water is less vigorous than that of sodium which has the least negative E0 value among the alkali metals. The reason is:

options.label: A
options.text: it is a strong reducing agent

questions.explanation: This behaviour of lithium is attributed to its small size and very high hydration energy. Li^+ has a maximum degree of hydration.

questions.question: When sodium is dissolved in liquid ammonia, a solution of deep blue colour is obtained. The colour of the solution is due to
questions.question_raw: When sodium is dissolved in liquid ammonia, a solution of deep blue colour is obtained. The colour of the solution is due to
questions.explanation:
The alkali metals dissolve in liquid ammonia giving deep blue solutions which are conducting in nature. The blue colour of the solution is due to the ammoniated electron which absorbs energy in the visible region of light and thus imparts blue colour to the solution.
$M+(x+y)NH_3\to[M(NH_3)_x]^+[e(NH_3)_y]^-$

questions.question: The alkali metals dissolve in liquid ammonia giving deep blue solutions. When they become diamagnetic, the solution turns into:
questions.question_raw: The alkali metals dissolve in liquid ammonia giving deep blue solutions. When they become diamagnetic, the solution turns into:
questions.explanation: When the solution becomes diamagnetic due to the formation of an electron cluster in which ammoniated electron with the opposite spin group. It changes its color to bronze.

questions.question: Lithium halides are somewhat covalent because
questions.question_raw: Lithium halides are somewhat covalent because

options.label: A
options.text: Li+ ion is very large in size and has high tendency to distort electron

options.label: B
options.text: Li+ ion is very small in size and has low tendency to distort electron

options.label: C
options.text: low polarisation capability of lithium ion

options.label: D
options.text: high polarisation capability of lithium ion

correct_option_texts: high polarisation capability of lithium ion
questions.explanation: Li^+ ion has a small size and maximum tendency to attract the electrons towards itself from the negative ion. This distortion of the electron cloud of the negative ion by the positive ion is known as polarization. Thus Li^+ has high polarization capability. Lithium iodide is the most covalent in nature.

questions.question: Choose the correct trend of the melting and boiling points in the alkali metal halides: MF, MCl, MI, MBr.
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct trend of the melting and boiling points in the alkali metal halides: MF, MCl, MI, MBr.
questions.explanation: Smaller is the size of anion less is the polarizing power of anion so greater is the ionic character hence greater will be the melting point.

questions.question: All alkali metal halides are soluble in water except:
questions.question_raw: All alkali metal halides are soluble in water except:
questions.explanation: Greater the lattice energy, the higher is the melting point of the MX (metal halide) and the lower is its solubility.

questions.question: Why KO_2 is paramagnetic?
questions.question_raw: Why KO2 is paramagnetic?

options.label: A
options.text: because of large size of the metal ion

options.label: B
options.text: because of one unpaired electron in ${\pi ^ * }$ 1p

options.label: C
options.text: because of oxygen atom in -1/2 oxidation state

correct_option_texts: because of oxygen atom in -1/2 oxidation state
questions.explanation: KO_2 is superoxide, K is in the +1 oxidation state, and O_2 has -1 oxidation state, thus each O has -1/2 oxidation state. So due to the presence of unpaired electron, it behaves as free radical. As we know that a molecule containing an unpaired electron is paramagnetic in nature. So KO_2 is paramagnetic in nature because of one unpaired electron in $\pi$*2p molecular orbital.

questions.question: Alkali metal chlorides donot form hydrates except for one metal. Choose from among the options given below.
questions.question_raw: Alkali metal chlorides donot form hydrates except for one metal. Choose from among the options given below.
questions.explanation: Lithium due to its high hydration energy (deliquescent) forms hydrate.

tests.testPaperName: The s Block Elements CBSE Online Test 03
tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Chemistry > The s-Block Elements (FA ONLY)

questions.question: In the preparation of sodium carbonate the by-products formed are:
questions.question_raw: In the preparation of sodium carbonate the by-products formed are:
questions.explanation:
Sodium carbonate is generally prepared by Solvay Process. First ammonium bicarbonate is prepared by passing CO_2 to a concentrated solution of sodium chloride saturated with ammonia, where ammonium carbonate followed by ammonium hydrogen carbonate is formed. The equations for the complete process may be written as : 
$2NH_3+H_2O+CO_2\to(NH_4)_2CO_3$
$(NH_4)_2CO_3+H_2O+CO_2\to2NH_4HCO_3$
$NH_4HCO_3+NaCl\to NH_4Cl+NaHCO_3$
Sodium hydrogen carbonate crystal separates. These are heated to give sodium carbonate. 
$2NaHCO_3\to Na_2CO_3+CO_2+H_2O$
In this process, NH_3 is recovered when the solution containing NH_4Cl is treated with Ca(OH)_2. Calcium chloride is obtained as a by-product.
$2NH_4Cl+Ca(OH)_2\to 2NH_3 + CaCl_2+H_2O$

questions.question: Washing soda is:
questions.question_raw: Washing soda is:
questions.explanation: Washing soda is Na_2CO_3.10H_2O i.e. sodium carbonate decahydrate. It is used in water softening, laundering and cleaning, manufacture of glass, etc.

questions.question: The common salt or table salt is:
questions.question_raw: The common salt or table salt is:
questions.explanation: Common salt is sodium chloride i.e. NaCl. It is generally obtained by the evaporation of seawater. It is used as table salt for domestic purposes.

questions.question: Caustic soda is:
questions.question_raw: Caustic soda is:
questions.explanation: Caustic Soda is Sodium hydroxide i.e. NaOH. Sodium hydroxide is generally prepared commercially by the electrolysis of sodium chloride in the Castner-Kellner cell. It is used in the manufacture of soap.

questions.question: Sodium hydrogen carbonate is known as:
questions.question_raw: Sodium hydrogen carbonate is known as:
questions.explanation: Sodium hydrogen carbonate i.e. NaHCO_3 is baking soda because it decomposes on heating to generate bubbles of carbon dioxide (leaving holes in cakes or pastries and making them light and fluffy).

questions.question: Potassium ions are the most abundant cations. They
questions.question_raw: Potassium ions are the most abundant cations. They

options.label: A
options.text: found primarily on the outside of cells.

options.label: B
options.text: have a role in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP.

options.label: C
options.text: inactivate many enzymes which help in penetration of cell membrane.

options.label: D
options.text: are the most abundant anions within cell fluids.

correct_option_texts: have a role in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP.
questions.explanation: Potassium ions are the most abundant cations. They activate many enzymes and participate in the oxidation of glucose to produce ATP.

questions.question: To acquire a noble gas configuration, alkaline earth metals lose:
questions.question_raw: To acquire a noble gas configuration, alkaline earth metals lose:
questions.explanation: Alkaline earth metals have two electrons in the s-orbital of the valence shell. Their general electronic configuration may be represented as [noble gas] ns^2. So they lose 2 electrons to attain noble gas configuration.

questions.question: Alkaline earth metals have a large size of the atoms. Therefore, they shows
questions.question_raw: Alkaline earth metals have a large size of the atoms. Therefore, they shows
questions.explanation: The reason is that the atoms of alkali metals are of large sizes. therefore, the outermost electron is for away from the nucleus and can be easily removed.

questions.question: Choose the correct order of increase in electropositivity of alkaline earth metals in the options given below:
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct order of increase in electropositivity of alkaline earth metals in the options given below:
questions.explanation: As we move down the group size increases so IE value decreases thus electropositivity also decreases (shielding effect also decreases as we go down the Group).

questions.question: Choose the correct order of increase in the ionic size of alkaline earth metals in the options given below:
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct order of increase in the ionic size of alkaline earth metals in the options given below:
questions.explanation: As we move down the group ionic radii increases.

questions.question: Beryllium carbonate is unstable and can be kept only in the atmosphere of:
questions.question_raw: Beryllium carbonate is unstable and can be kept only in the atmosphere of:
questions.explanation:
Beryllium carbonate is reported to be unstable under normal conditions and decomposes into BeO and CO_2 so it has to be stored under CO_2 to maintain equilibrium.
BeCO_​​​3 $ \leftrightarrow $ BeO+CO_2

questions.question: Radium salts are used in:
questions.question_raw: Radium salts are used in:
questions.explanation: Radium salts are used in radiotherapy, for example, in the treatment of cancer.

questions.question: Choose the correct order of decrease in hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metals in the options given below.
questions.question_raw: Choose the correct order of decrease in hydration enthalpies of alkaline earth metals in the options given below.
questions.explanation: As the size increases due to increase in ionic radii , hydration energy decreases.

questions.question: Why does the solubility of alkaline earth metal carbonates and sulphates in water decrease down the group?
questions.question_raw: Why does the solubility of alkaline earth metal carbonates and sulphates in water decrease down the group?

options.label: A
options.text: hydration enthalpy decreases down the group

options.label: B
options.text: lattice enthalpy decreases down the group

options.label: C
options.text: size of anions being much shorterer compared to cations

correct_option_texts: hydration enthalpy decreases down the group
questions.explanation: The solubility of sulphates and carbonates decreases down the group because of decrease in hydration energy which is insufficient to overcome the lattice energy of ions.

questions.question: Calcium oxide is prepared on a commercial scale by using:
questions.question_raw: Calcium oxide is prepared on a commercial scale by using:
questions.explanation:
Rotary kiln is used for the calcination of limestone (CaCO_3) to produce calcium oxide (quicklime).
CaCO_3 $\longleftrightarrow$ CaO+CO_2

tests.testPaperName: The s Block Elements CBSE Online Test 04

questions.question: Heating a pulverised mixture of limestone and clay in a rotary kiln is used in the manufacture of:
questions.question_raw: Heating a pulverised mixture of limestone and clay in a rotary kiln is used in the manufacture of:
questions.explanation: Heating a pulverised mixture of limestone and clay in a rotary kiln is used in the manufacture of Portland cement. Cement is a product obtained by combining a material rich in lime, CaO with other materials such as clay which contains silica, SiO_2 along with the oxides of aluminium, iron, and magnesium.

questions.question: Cement is an industrially important compound of:
questions.question_raw: Cement is an industrially important compound of:
questions.explanation: Cement is an important compound of Calcium. Cement is a product obtained by combining a material rich in lime, CaO with other materials such as clay which contains silica, SiO_2 long with the oxides of aluminium, iron, and magnesium.

questions.question: The alkali metals are low melting. Which of the following alkali metal is expected to melt if the room temperature rises to ${\text{3}}0^\circ {\text{C}}?$
questions.question_raw: The alkali metals are low melting. Which of the following alkali metal is expected to melt if the room temperature rises to {tex}{\text{3}}0^\circ {\text{C}}?{/tex}
questions.explanation: Cs has very low melting point (24.44 degree Celsius). It can melt even with the heat of the hand.

questions.question: Alkali metals react with water vigorously to form hydroxides and dihydrogen.Which of the following alkali metals reacts with water least vigorously?
questions.question_raw: Alkali metals react with water vigorously to form hydroxides and dihydrogen.Which of the following alkali metals reacts with water least vigorously?
questions.explanation: Li reacts with water least vigorously, since the density of Li is only about half that of water,so it floats on the surface and ultimately dissappears,giving off H_2 gas

questions.question: The reducing power of a metal depends on various factors. Suggest the factor which makes Li, the strongest reducing agent in aqueous solution.
questions.question_raw: The reducing power of a metal depends on various factors. Suggest the factor which makes Li, the strongest reducing agent in aqueous solution.
questions.explanation:
Lithium (Li) is the strongest reducing agent in aqueous solution because of its extremely high hydration enthalpy.
 When Li loses an electron to form Li⁺, the small ion with high charge density gets very strongly hydrated in water.
 This large negative hydration enthalpy compensates for its high ionization energy.
 As a result, the overall reduction potential becomes highly negative, making Li a very strong reducing agent.
So, hydration enthalpy is the dominant factor here.

questions.question: Metal carbonates decompose on heating to give metal oxide and carbon dioxide. Which of the metal carbonates is most stable thermally?
questions.question_raw: Metal carbonates decompose on heating to give metal oxide and carbon dioxide. Which of the metal carbonates is most stable thermally?
questions.explanation: BaCO_3 is very stable due to the size compatibility factor. A larger cation can stabilize a larger anion. Group 2 elements become more thermally stable down the group.

questions.question: The diagonal relationship exists between:
questions.question_raw: The diagonal relationship exists between:
questions.explanation: 1^st element of 1^st group and the second element of 2^nd group exist in the diagonal relationship due to their nearly same polarizing power.

questions.question: Metals form basic hydroxides. Which of the following metal hydroxide is the least basic?
questions.question_raw: Metals form basic hydroxides. Which of the following metal hydroxide is the least basic?
questions.explanation: Due to small size of Mg. Mg-O become more stronger hence tendency of OH to leave decreases thus basic character decreases.

questions.question: Some of the Group 2 metal halides are covalent and soluble in organic solvents. Among the following metal halides, the one which is soluble in ethanol is
questions.question_raw: Some of the Group 2 metal halides are covalent and soluble in organic solvents. Among the following metal halides, the one which is soluble in ethanol is
questions.explanation: BeCl_2 is soluble in ethanol. As BeCl_2_ is hygroscopic it is soluble in many polar solvent.

questions.question: The order of decreasing ionisation enthalpy in alkali metals is
questions.question_raw: The order of decreasing ionisation enthalpy in alkali metals is
questions.explanation: As we more down the group size increases and also ionization energy decreases.

questions.question: The solubility of metal halides depends on their nature, lattice enthalpy and hydration enthalpy of the individual ions. Amongst fluorides of alkali metals,the lowest solubility of LiF in water is due to
questions.question_raw: The solubility of metal halides depends on their nature, lattice enthalpy and hydration enthalpy of the individual ions. Amongst fluorides of alkali metals,the lowest solubility of LiF in water is due to

options.label: A
options.text: High hydration enthalpy for lithium ion.

options.label: D
options.text: Low ionisation enthalpy of lithium atom

questions.explanation: LiF has very high lattice energy which cannot be compensated by Hydration Energy.

questions.question: Amphoteric hydroxides react with both alkalies and acids. Which of the following Group 2 metal hydroxides is soluble in sodium hydroxide?
questions.question_raw: Amphoteric hydroxides react with both alkalies and acids. Which of the following Group 2 metal hydroxides is soluble in sodium hydroxide?
questions.explanation: BaOH_2 is soluble in NaOH. Since Be act as amphoteric unlike other groups 2 elements.

questions.question: In the synthesis of sodium carbonate, the recovery of ammonia is done by treating NH_4Cl with Ca(OH)_2. The by-product obtained in this process is
questions.question_raw: In the synthesis of sodium carbonate, the recovery of ammonia is done by treating NH4Cl with Ca(OH)2. The by-product obtained in this process is
questions.explanation:
By product obtained in this process is CaCl_​2​​.
NH_4Cl + Ca(OH)_2 $\to$ CaCl_2 + 2NH_3+H_2O

questions.question: The nitrate of which of the following alkaline earth metals crystallizes as anhydrous salt?
questions.question_raw: The nitrate of which of the following alkaline earth metals crystallizes as anhydrous salt?
questions.explanation: Magnesium nitrate crystallizes with six molecules of water, whereas barium nitrate crystallizes as the anhydrous salt. This shows a decreasing tendency to form hydrates with increasing size and decreasing hydration enthalpy.

questions.question: By adding gypsum to cement
questions.question_raw: By adding gypsum to cement

options.label: A
options.text: setting time of cement becomes less.

questions.explanation: When mixed with water, the setting of cement takes place to give a hard mass. This is due to the hydration of the molecules of the constituents and their rearrangement. The purpose of adding gypsum is only to slow down the process of setting of the cement so that it gets sufficiently hardened

questions.question: Dead burnt plaster is
questions.question_raw: Dead burnt plaster is
questions.explanation: Above 393 K, no water of crystallisation is left and anhydrous calcium sulphate, CaSO_4 is formed. This is known as ‘dead burnt plaster’.

questions.question: The suspension of slaked lime in water is known as:
questions.question_raw: The suspension of slaked lime in water is known as:
questions.explanation: Slaked lime is Ca(OH)_2. Suspension of slaked lime in water is known as milk of lime because it looks just like milk.

questions.question: Which of the following elements does not form hydride by direct heating with dihydrogen?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following elements does not form hydride by direct heating with dihydrogen?
questions.explanation: Be does not form hydride directly by reacting with hydrogen. It possesses a polymeric bridge structure with H_2 between two atoms.

questions.question: The formula of soda ash is _________.
questions.question_raw: The formula of soda ash is _________.
questions.explanation: Above 373K, sodium carbonate monohydrate becomes completely anhydrous and changes to a white powder called soda ash i.e. Na_2CO_3.

questions.question: A substance which gives brick red flame and breaks down on heating to give oxygen and a brown gas is
questions.question_raw: A substance which gives brick red flame and breaks down on heating to give oxygen and a brown gas is
questions.explanation: Ca give brick red flame.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about Ca(OH)_2?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following statements is true about Ca(OH)2?

options.label: A
options.text: It is a light blue solid

options.label: B
options.text: It does not possess disinfectant property.

options.label: C
options.text: It is used in the preparation of bleaching powder

options.label: D
options.text: It is used in the manufacture of cement.

correct_option_texts: It is used in the preparation of bleaching powder
questions.explanation: Ca(OH)_2 is a white amorphous powder which is used in the manufacture of bleaching powder. It is used in the white wash because of its disinfectant nature.

questions.question: A chemical A is used for the preparation of washing soda to recover ammonia. When CO_2 is bubbled through an aqueous solution of A, the solution turns milky. It is used in white-washing due to disinfectant nature. What is the chemical formula of A?
questions.question_raw: A chemical A is used for the preparation of washing soda to recover ammonia. When CO2 is bubbled through an aqueous solution of A, the solution turns milky. It is used in white-washing due to disinfectant nature. What is the chemical formula of A?
questions.explanation:
Ca(OH)_2 is used for the preparation of washing soda to recover ammonia. 
$2NH_4Cl+Ca(OH)_2\to CaCl_2+2NH_3+H_2O$ 
When CO_2 is passed through Ca(OH)_2, CaCO_3 is formed which turns the solution milky.
Ca(OH)_2 + CO_2 $\longrightarrow$ CaCO_3

questions.question: Dehydration of hydrates of halides of calcium, barium and strontium i.e., ${\text{CaC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{6}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},$ ${\text{BaC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}.{\text{2 }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},{\text{ SrC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}.{\text{2 }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},$ can be achieved by heating. These become wet on keeping in air. Which of the following statements is correct about these halides?
questions.question_raw: Dehydration of hydrates of halides of calcium, barium and strontium i.e., {tex}{\text{CaC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{6}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},{/tex} {tex}{\text{BaC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}.{\text{2 }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},{\text{ SrC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}.{\text{2 }}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}},{/tex} can be achieved by heating. These become wet on keeping in air. Which of the following statements is correct about these halides?

options.label: A
options.text: Tendency to form hydrate decreases from calcium to barium

questions.explanation: All of these statements are coorect as these halides absorb water and due to decrease in the hydration energy down the group tendency to from hydrates decreases.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statements from the following.
 Beryllium is not readily attacked by acids because of the presence of an oxide film on the surface of the metal.
 Beryllium sulphate is insoluble in water as the greater lattice enthalpy factor of Be^2+ overcomes the hydration enthalpy.
 Beryllium exhibits a coordination number of more than four.
 Beryllium oxide is purely acidic in nature.
questions.question_raw:
Choose the correct statements from the following.

  1. Beryllium is not readily attacked by acids because of the presence of an oxide film on the surface of the metal.
  2. Beryllium sulphate is insoluble in water as the greater lattice enthalpy factor of Be2+ overcomes the hydration enthalpy.
  3. Beryllium exhibits a coordination number of more than four.
  4. Beryllium oxide is purely acidic in nature.
questions.explanation: The surface of the Beryllium metal is covered with a thin layer of oxides that helps protect the metal from attack by acid, but powdered beryllium metal dissolves readily in dilute acids.

questions.question: Identify the correct formula of halides of alkaline earth metals from the following:
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct formula of halides of alkaline earth metals from the following:
questions.explanation: The tendency to form halide hydrates gradually decreases down the group.${\text{BaC}}{{\text{l}}_{\text{2}}}.{\text{2}}{{\text{H}}_{\text{2}}}{\text{O}}$ i.e., Barium chloride dihydrate exists.

questions.question: Several sodium compounds find use in industries. Which of the following compounds is/ are used for textile industry?
questions.question_raw: Several sodium compounds find use in industries. Which of the following compounds is/ are used for textile industry?
questions.explanation: Sodium carbonate i.e. Na_2CO_3​​​​​​​ is used in paper, paints and textile industries.

questions.question: Which of the following compounds are readily soluble in water?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following compounds are readily soluble in water?
questions.explanation: BeSO_4 being unstable is readily soluble in water. Also, the hydration energy of Be is high due to the small size.

questions.question: When Zeolite, which is hydrated sodium aluminium silicate is treated with hard water, the sodium ions are exchanged with which of the following ion(s)?
questions.question_raw: When Zeolite, which is hydrated sodium aluminium silicate is treated with hard water, the sodium ions are exchanged with which of the following ion(s)?
questions.explanation: When hard water containing Mg^2+ and Ca^2+ passes through a bed of sodium zeolite, then the sodium ions are replaced with by the calcium and magnesium ions. This is used in the water softening process.

questions.question: Which of the following are the correct reasons for anomalous behaviour of lithium?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following are the correct reasons for anomalous behaviour of lithium?

options.label: C
options.text: Exceptionally small size of its atom

correct_option_texts: Exceptionally small size of its atom
questions.explanation: The anomalous behaviour of lithium is due to the : (i) exceptionally small size of its atom and ion, and (ii) high polarising power (i.e., charge/ radius ratio). As a result, there is an increased covalent character of lithium compounds which is responsible for their solubility in organic solvents.

questions.question: Magnesium do not impart any colour to the flame because:
questions.question_raw: Magnesium do not impart any colour to the flame because:

options.label: B
options.text: The electrons in magnesium are too loosly bound to get excited.

options.label: C
options.text: The electrons in magnesium are too strongly bound to get excited.

correct_option_texts: The electrons in magnesium are too strongly bound to get excited.
questions.explanation: Electrons in Mg are held closer to nucleus as it is very small in size. So does not get excited by the energy provided by flame.
